creator: vova@fbsd.ru
text:
fyi - even adding frr_flags="--limit-fds=10000" to /etc/rc.conf 

does not help completely, but decrease number of warnings:

# service frr restart
Stopping bgpd.
Waiting for PIDS: 84549.
Stopping staticd.
Waiting for PIDS: 84543.
Stopping ospfd.
Waiting for PIDS: 84538.
Stopping zebra.
Waiting for PIDS: 84532.
Checking zebra.conf
2024/07/09 21:08:42 ZEBRA: [YDG3W-JND95] FD Limit set: 219780 is stupidly large.  Is this what you intended?  Consider using --limit-fds also limiting size to 100000
2024/07/09 21:08:42 ZEBRA: [NNACN-54BDA][EC 4043309110] Disabling MPLS support (no kernel support)
OK
Starting zebra.
2024/07/09 21:08:42 ZEBRA: [NNACN-54BDA][EC 4043309110] Disabling MPLS support (no kernel support)
Checking ospfd.conf
2024/07/09 21:08:42 OSPF: [YDG3W-JND95] FD Limit set: 219780 is stupidly large.  Is this what you intended?  Consider using --limit-fds also limiting size to 100000
OK
Starting ospfd.
Checking staticd.conf
confchk staticd ignored
Starting staticd.
Checking bgpd.conf
2024/07/09 21:08:43 BGP: [YDG3W-JND95] FD Limit set: 219780 is stupidly large.  Is this what you intended?  Consider using --limit-fds also limiting size to 100000
2024/07/09 21:08:43 BGP: [YDG3W-JND95] FD Limit set: 219780 is stupidly large.  Is this what you intended?  Consider using --limit-fds also limiting size to 100000
2024/07/09 21:08:43 BGP: [YDG3W-JND95] FD Limit set: 219780 is stupidly large.  Is this what you intended?  Consider using --limit-fds also limiting size to 100000
OK
Starting bgpd.
